channel.title: Staff on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science

item.description:
Steven Winship

Software Developer and Systems Administrator, Product Development

Steven is working on the Dataverse Project.

item.title: Rand Kiessig
item.description:
Rand Kiessig

Manager, IQSS Computer Lab Services
email rkiessig@iq.harvard.edu

Rand Kiessig joined IQSS in May 2012 and has led IQSS Computer Lab Services since 2015. A California native, Rand has lived across the U.S. — from Washington State to North Carolina to Massachusetts — with stints in Mexico and Spain along the way. Before coming to Harvard, he held a range of technical roles at organizations including Microsoft, Verio, Data General, and Iron Mountain.

item.title: Maria Martins
item.description:
Maria Martins

Executive Assistant
email mmartins@iq.harvard.edu

Maria Joined IQSS in March of 2016.  Prior to IQSS, she worked as a Executive Assistant in the Office for Sponsored Programs (OSP) for eight years.  Before Harvard, Maria worked for The Inn at Harvard as the Office Manager and at the Hilton Back Bay and Hilton Logan Airport as a Sales and Catering Assistant.  Maria holds a Bachelors of Science in Business Administration from Newbury College.  In her free time, Maria enjoys spending time with her family, running, and volunteering.

item.title: Hongsu Henry Wang
item.description:
Hongsu Wang

Associate of IQSS
email hongsuwang@fas.harvard.edu

Hongsu Wang is the senior project manager of the China Biographical Database project (CBDB), which is jointly owned by Harvard University, Academia Sinica, and Peking University. As CBDB's project manager, he developed the database's OCR proofreading platform, a cross-catalogue query system for digital sources, and a KML path generator tool. His publications include Structuring, Recording, and Analyzing Historical Networks in the China Biographical Database(Journal of Historical Network Research, 2021), Harvesting Big Biographical Data for Chinese History: The China Biographical Database(Journal of Chinese History, 2020), Harvesting Big Biographical Data for Chinese History: The China Biographical Database(Journal of Chinese History, 2020). Created and continuously maintain the CBDB open-source community https://github.com/orgs/cbdb-project/repositories

item.title: Gabrielle Stone
item.description:
Gabrielle Stone

Events Coordinator
email gstone@iq.harvard.edu

Gabrielle is a professional event planner who has worked with Harvard University for 10 years. She gradutated from the University of Connecticut with a Bachelor's degree in English and has a Certificate of Special Studies in Administration and Management from Harvard Extension School, focusing on nonprofit management, marketing and public relations, museum administration, and fundraising. Her previous career was in publishing.

item.title: Emily Lawrence
item.description:
Emily Lawrence

Linux Systems Administrator for Research Computing, HMDC
email elawrence@hmdc.harvard.edu

Emily majored in Electronic Media, Arts and Communications at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, and worked at BU for six years before joining IQSS in 2016.

item.title: Dwayne Liburd
item.description:
Dwayne Liburd

Library Assistant, Data Acquisition and Archiving, Data Science
email dliburd@hmdc.harvard.edu

Dwayne Liburd is the Data Technology Support Associate in Data Acquisition and Archiving. His responsibilities include assisting the Manager of Data Acquisition, providing assistance on using Dataverse services, and providing user support for Dataverse. Dwayne also maintains the Henry A. Murray Research Archive Dataverse. He performs the cataloging of the metadata, storage of data related to the archive, and data processing, preservation, and dissemination of the original holdings of the Archive.

item.title: Devika Jain
item.description:
Devika Jain

GIS Data Science Manager
email kakkar@fas.harvard.edu

At CGA, Devika Jain leads initiatives in GeoAI, Spatial Data Science and Big Data using High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud Computing. She has led the development of impactful solutions, including TSGI (a UN-recognized spatial well-being metric), RINX- a transformative tool for the handling of raster big data , and a novel K-NN method enabling the first individual-level analysis of partisan segregation in USA. She has also led the development of university-wide Spatial Data Infrastructure (SDI) at Harvard.

item.title: Cameron Harvey
item.description:
Cameron Harvey

IT Client Support Services Associate
email help@iq.harvard.edu

Cameron Harvey joined IQSS in 2018 as an IT Client Services Support Specialist. He provides members of the IQSS community with IT desktop support. Previosly Cameron worked in the role of IT Systems Administrator for an EMS service provider, as well as IT Systems Support for a medical firm.

item.title: Bailey Moore
item.description:
Bailey Moore

IT Client Support Services Associate
email baileymoore@iq.harvard.edu

Bailey Moore joined IQSS in 2024 as an IT Client Services Support Specialist. She went to school for Computer Networking at Lamar Institute of Technology. She grew up in Texas, moved to New York, and now is in Boston. Previously Bailey worked as a Network Administrator for a School District, and IT Help Desk for Neches FCU before that.

item.title: Gustavo Durand
item.description:
Gustavo Durand

Dataverse Technical Lead / Architect
email gdurand@iq.harvard.edu

Gustavo Durand is the Technical Lead and Architect of Dataverse, an open source data repository platform developed by IQSS at Harvard University. Gustavo has been on Dataverse in many roles since its inception and in his current role, leads the architecture and technical design of the platform, reviews code from internal and and external developers, assists the Dataverse Community contributors, and overall works closely with the Development Project Manager. He began his Java programming career at Cambridge Technology Partners in 1997 and has over 20 total years experience as a software developer and consultant.

item.title: Keri Lemasters
item.description:
Keri Lemasters

Senior Sponsored Research Administrator
email klemasters@iq.harvard.edu

Keri joined the sponsored finance team at IQSS in February of 2019 and has worked in sponsored administration for nine years. Keri supports several principal investigators affiliated with IQSS in pre-award and post-award services for federal and private foundation awards.
